concerns.text: Phase 6 (Tasks T051-T054) introduces 'confidence_latency' and a four-way interaction model. This violates FR-004 (three-way interaction only) and the Spec's defined outcome (post-task belief rating). The task list implements unapproved scope creep, violating the constraint that tasks must not silently relax or expand the spec's defined mechanism.

Task T028 requires the report to 'assert the causal link within the controlled experimental design (synthetic ground truth)'. This conflates synthetic validation with empirical causal proof, violating Constitution Principle VII (Outcome-Neutral Validation) and FR-006 which mandates causal framing 'given the experimental design' (implying real stimuli), not synthetic parameters.

Tasks T035 and T036 implement a sensitivity analysis sweeping significance levels (alpha). FR-005 and SC-003 explicitly restrict robustness checks to sweeping 'fixation duration cutoffs {50ms, 100ms, 150ms}'. The alpha sweep is an orphan task not authorized by the spec, violating the constraint against silent scope expansion.

Task T032 states the input for robustness analysis is 'data/derived/regression_results.csv' (static output). FR-005 and US-3 require 'sweeping' thresholds to measure variation, which necessitates re-running the regression model with new parameters. A task relying on static results cannot satisfy the acceptance criterion of measuring variation across thresholds.

Task T006 hardcodes I-DT dispersion (100px) and I-VT velocity (30 deg/s). FR-001 only mandates a 100ms duration threshold. Hardcoding specific algorithm parameters not defined in the spec risks violating the 'Reproducibility' principle if these defaults do not match the 'standard' algorithms referenced in the spec without explicit justification in the spec.
